hero_build.name: Chrome's Rapid Haze
hero_build.description: I’ve Returned From Holiday, Updated for the New Year! – (Chrome’s) Rapid Haze is a gun-centric, gank focused roamer build that exceeds in lockdown kill potential. We rapidly – haha – scale our offensive, defensive, and technical skills with special care, creating an effective mixture of high damage, strong sustain, and helpful tools. Visit the full written guide in Deadlock’s official Discord server for further details and to ask any questions :)

mods.annotation: Sell for late game tech.
Sell for Spirit Armor.
It's best to sell Ammo Scavenger or Headshot Booster before this when choked on slots.
If laning against a hero with large, telegraphed damage such as Geist Bomb, Viscous Splatter, or Warden Cage, buy this 3rd.
If you’re suffering from large chunks of damage or need to quickly regen in-lane after a tough 1v1, pick this up. Sell for Combat Barrier.

To put pressure on your opponent in lane. This is well applied in winning lanes to capitalize on momentum, and against lane opponents who are fond of standing out from cover regardless of advantaged / disadvantaged lane state.
QSR is most effectively used at the end of your first clip to extend your damage whether against an enemy, tower, or camp. It can also be used to extend the buff of Kinetic Dash to two clips.

mod_categories.name: Ganking Tech (Read Annotations)       Runners ↓      Leechers ↓     Gunners ↓

mods.annotation: Our default ganking relic, an excellent tool for locking down enemies into your Bullet Dance or otherwise.
Against Tanks & Leeches like Abrams, Shiv, and Geist. Use this during a gank or to push them away in a team fight.
If your opponent has high weapon damage output, use this during a gank to create a one-sided exchange. Best against Wraith, Ivy, McGinnis, Talon, etc.
If you are getting heavily CC'd in Bullet Dance or otherwise, I advise you buy this before the other T4 options. Unstoppable does a lot to keep us alive in Late Game.
When facing heavy spirit users, which is frequent, new Silencer shuts them down completely.
A straightforward “nerf” relic for our late game, it’s impossible to go wrong with Inhibitor. Especially helpful for outliving fights against metal skinned enemies.
Our strongest M1 & Ult damage increase after Spiritual Overflow, best to pick this up if you are not in need of a tech pick.
A strong pairing with gun or Ult to briefly increase your sustain and keep enemies locked around you. If incoming CC is low, this can supersede Unstoppable.
If their team has a massive carry, Curse is sometimes a requirement to kill them and turn a teamfight.
An essential purchase at some point, it’s important to sense when that is exactly in the match. Frequently around the 11-15 minute mark.
Another essential purchase, but can be picked up much later in the round. Any time after Spiritual Overflow is ideal.

mod_categories.description: Check out the Discord Guide for details and to ask any questions.

hero_build.name: Chrome's Rapid Haze
hero_build.description: UPDATED 4/24! – (Chrome’s) Rapid Haze is a gun-centric, gank focused roamer build that exceeds in lockdown kill potential. We rapidly – haha – scale our offensive, defensive, and technical skills with special care, creating an effective mixture of high damage, strong sustain, and helpful tools. Visit the full written guide in Deadlock’s official Discord server for further details and to ask any questions :)

mods.annotation: When taking consistent chip damage, Extra Regen is essential in keeping your health pool high and resistant to pressure.
It's best to sell Extra Spirit or Headshot Booster before this when choked on slots.
If laning against a hero with large, telegraphed damage such as Geist Bomb, Viscous Splatter, or Warden Cage, buy this 3rd.
If you’re suffering from large chunks of damage or need to quickly regen in-lane after a tough 1v1, pick this up. Sell for Combat Barrier.

To put pressure on your opponent in lane. This is well applied in winning lanes to capitalize on momentum, and against lane opponents who are fond of standing out from cover regardless of advantaged / disadvantaged lane state.

QSR is most effectively used at the end of your first clip to extend your damage whether against an enemy, tower, or camp. It can also be used to extend the buff of Fleetfoot’s extended mag to two clips.
A relic that greatly enables our rotation speed, mid-fight maneuverability, and general sustained damage output. Use it when jungling in tandem with QSR!

mod_categories.name: Spirit Tech (Read Annotations) Runners ↓        Gunners ↓     Smoke T3 ↓

mods.annotation: Our default ganking relic, an excellent tool for locking down enemies into your Bullet Dance or otherwise. Wait to throw Slowing Hex until after you’ve daggered them midway to ensure they can’t escape.
If your opponent has high weapon damage output, use this during a gank to create a one-sided exchange. Best against Wraith, Mirage, McGinnis, Talon, etc.
Purchase IMMEDIATELY when unlocking Smoke T3
Lucky Shot shows its strength against teams that are tightly-knit and less vulnerable, whether that be their comp or their playstyle. It struggles against high resists.
A strong relic to pierce through the pervasive Bullet Armor present in the current meta. Crippling Headshot can out damage Lucky Shot if the enemy team has built substantial resistances.
An essential upgrade that should be bought whenever possible as it ultimately enables our survivability during important mid-late teamfights. I try to get this no later than the 20-minute mark!

hero_build.name: Chrome's Rapid Haze
hero_build.description:
Updated 5/19 - More big improvements! Relic exchanges and refinements to make us stronger, quicker. Overhauled Annotations!

Chrome's Rapid Haze aims to maximize Haze's ability to create one-sided, unfair exchanges. Strong Fixation and Dagger damage is paired with crushing debuffs to confirm high value kills. Check the guide in the official discord for updates and ask questions :)

mods.annotation: Prioritize proccing off cooldown, spend downtime clearing waves.
Incredibly strong harass tool. 100 Spirit damage on a single proc. Shoot them at distances you’d otherwise not bother with if it means you could get a proc.
If taking chip damage in lane and need to stay healthy.
If taking big chunks of damage from ability combos (Bebop hook, Shiv dash, etc.) pop Healing Rite and go break statues / boxes while regenerating.
If you're in a very melee brawly lane, this can be a game changer. Especially strong into a Viscous spamming Puddle Punches.
Best used when springing fights onto enemies out of smoke, no longer a "stamina saver. I advise you put some thought into your stamina usage and rely on Smoke Bomb’s speed for rotations.
Use at the end of your first magazine to extend your damage output during fights and farming.
A cornerstone ganking tool. Delay its application for as long as possible. I advise you burn a mag, dagger, and apply it while they’re slept.
Sheds all triggered and activated buffs (Metal Skin, Weapon Shielding, Infuser, Mercurial Magnum, Scourge) as well as hero-specific buffs (Full Auto, Cube, Power Surge) use reactively.
Best used for high burst damage in gank engagements and as a health “re-up” in teamfights. This gets shutdown hard by Spirit Burn, try to wait it out.
To counter a Plated Armor pickup on the enemy team, be checking the HUD often to try and react to their purchase before the fight happens.
A strong universal boon to our Gun and Dagger output. Hard to go wrong with this, but there may be more specialized and effective choices.
For absolutely nuking single enemies, be sure to pop Echo Shard before shooting after the first dagger to cancel the animation. An exceptional picking tool, it's basically a Krill Combo.
Either by daggering or stacking Tesla / Fixation, Spirit Burn breaks Leech heroes. A low commitment relic that can have a large impact. Only buy if nobody else on your team has it.
Excellently helpful for large teamfights and sinking ability-heavy heroes in ganks.
Enables permanent map uptime, a frequent purchase in midgame. Do not buy if your team has no map control.
If in need of extreme maneuverability. I advise you to become more disciplined with your stamina usage during Late Game as to avoid having to buy this.
To cleanse overwhelming and dangerous debuffs such as Spirit Burn, Bebop Bombs, Warden Cage, etc. No longer works on Affliction and Telekinesis.
If you're in the position to apply this adequately during teamfights, Inhibitor can be very impactful.
Buy for late game when big CC is a huge issue, I prefer this for Haze over Counterspell.
Incredibly useful into high-burst Spirit teams such as Yamato, Bebop, or Talon.
Buy if you have free slots when grabbing Smoke T3. Can be ignored otherwise.

Used in tandem with Ult or your gun to shutdown and kill enemies. An essential Late Game relic for Haze.

Especially great against a well-fed Pocket, Bebop, or Warden. Cleanses more “debuffs” than you may realize. Bebop Bombs, Ticking Warden Cage, etc.
A fun pairing with gun or Ult to briefly increase your sustain and keep enemies locked around you.
For a huge burst of Damage & Sustain in Ult against tanky enemies like an Ulting Yamato or Ulting Warden.

hero_build.name: Chrome's Rapid Haze
hero_build.description:
Updated 5/28 - Adjusted our midgame path and putting a prio on Curse as it greatly enables Haze with ganks (in tandem with big slows)

Chrome's Rapid Haze aims to maximize Haze's ability to create one-sided, unfair exchanges. Strong Fixation and Dagger damage is paired with crushing debuffs to confirm high value kills. Check the guide in the official discord for updates and ask questions :)

mods.annotation: If taking chip damage in lane and need to stay healthy.
If taking big chunks of damage from ability combos (Bebop hook, Shiv dash, etc.) pop Healing Rite and go break statues / boxes while regenerating.
If you're in a very melee brawly lane, this can be a game changer. Especially strong into a Viscous spamming Puddle Punches.
Best used when springing fights onto enemies out of smoke, no longer a "stamina saver. I advise you put some thought into your stamina usage and rely on Smoke Bomb’s speed for rotations.
Use at the end of your first magazine to extend your damage output during fights and farming.
A cornerstone ganking tool. Delay its application for as long as possible. I advise you burn a mag, dagger, and apply it while they’re slept.
Sheds all triggered and activated buffs (Metal Skin, Weapon Shielding, Infuser, Mercurial Magnum, Scourge) as well as hero-specific buffs (Full Auto, Cube, Power Surge) use reactively.
For absolutely nuking single enemies, be sure to pop Echo Shard before shooting after the first dagger to cancel the animation. An exceptional picking tool, it's basically a Krill Combo.
To counter a Plated Armor pickup on the enemy team, be checking the HUD often to try and react to their purchase before the fight happens.
Best used for high burst damage in gank engagements and as a health “re-up” in teamfights. This gets shutdown hard by Spirit Burn, try to wait it out.
Either by daggering or stacking Tesla / Fixation, Spirit Burn breaks Leech heroes. A low commitment relic that can have a large impact. Only buy if nobody else on your team has it.
Enables permanent map uptime, a frequent purchase in midgame. Do not buy if your team has no map control.
If in need of extreme maneuverability. I advise you to become more disciplined with your stamina usage during Late Game as to avoid having to buy this.
To cleanse overwhelming and dangerous debuffs such as Spirit Burn, Bebop Bombs, Warden Cage, etc. No longer works on Affliction and Telekinesis.
If you're in the position to apply this adequately during teamfights, Inhibitor can be very impactful.
Buy for late game when big CC is a huge issue, I prefer this for Haze over Counterspell.
Incredibly useful into high-burst Spirit teams such as Yamato, Bebop, or Talon.
Buy if you have free slots when grabbing Smoke T3. Can be ignored otherwise.

We rapidly – haha – scale our offensive, defensive, and technical skills with special care, creating an effective mixture of high damage, strong sustain, and helpful tools. hero_build.name: Chrome's Rapid Haze
hero_build.description:
FURTHER FURTHER IMPROVEMENTS 8/7 - Giving us more freedom on the Extra Health upgrade and giving better tools for midgame with Fleetfoot and Toxic Bullets. #gethazeoutofthejungle

This build is now fully annotated! Give them a read for insight on timing and use cases.

mods.annotation: Best used when springing fights onto enemies out of smoke, no longer a "stamina saver. I advise you put some thought into your stamina usage and rely on Smoke Bomb’s speed for rotations.
If taking chip damage in lane and need to stay healthy.
If taking big chunks of damage from ability combos (Bebop hook, Shiv dash, etc.) pop Healing Rite and go break statues / boxes while regenerating.
If you're competent and able to properly poke enemy players in lane, this is a straight-up superior buy over Extra Regen. If they're playing super safe, it might not be ideal.
Use dagger at the end of your first mag to extend your damage in a gank if you didn’t need dagger to initiate. Use at the end of a mag when split pushing and stealing jungle.
This enables us to take gank damage trades and is basically a 30% health amp. I get this before Slowing Bullets often, but you can rush it into Spirit Burst lanes (Bebop, Krill, Wraith, etc.)
Use for rotating and rapid repositioning in fights.

This is our core gank enabler. Against slippery heroes with quick instant mobility like Lash, Vindicta, Infernus, Pocket, I apply this as I engage. If they’re not so quick I save it for halfway throug
Our primary T4 spike that amps our damage while keeping us healthy enough to keep fighting after a damage trade. Struggles into Spirit Burn.
I buy this frequently when I need to stack the odds in my favor for burst engagements past 25 minutes or so. Colossus keeps us healthy enough to win a high damage trade.
If you want a moderate upgrade to Slowing Hex, Wave is excellent. This is an especially strong tool into deathball comps.
I often sell Slowing Hex for Curse past 30 minutes. Communicate when you Curse someone in a team fight. Save it for big channel Ults if they have any.
If your team has adequate map control / wave pressure, Fortitude is a great pickup. HOWEVER, If your team doesn't have that control, you won't get the value to make it worth.
Buy for late game when big CC is a huge issue, I prefer this for Haze over Counterspell.
Incredibly useful into high-burst Spirit teams such as Yamato, Bebop, or Talon. Insane Late Game spirit resist item if you are well fed and able to push people around.
Anti Leechboss tank item that takes a lot of thought to use properly. Great to break down Mercurial Mag, Colossus, Scorn, etc.
A requirement into Plated Armor, but we still greatly struggle into Plated Armor regardless.
My preferred Debuff tool over Inhibitor for late game, as we are largely concerned with Spirit damage dealers.
Our "more damage" option that, sadly, overtakes Lucky Shot in performance. I'd get Armor Piercing Rounds before this.
To cleanse overwhelming and dangerous debuffs such as Spirit Burn, Bebop Bombs, Warden Cage, Vindicta Stake, etc. No longer works on Affliction and Telekinesis.

mod_categories.name: IMPROVEMENTS 8/7
mod_categories.description: Adjusting our midgame relic pickups to maximize our abilities in Midgame and give us some extended damage impact into Lategame. Very happy with where it's at currently! Shoot me any questions or comments in the #haze chat or in my guide threads <3

mod_categories.name: Gameplan
mod_categories.description: We play fully active with little / no teamside farming (sometimes Slots). Take trades in lane and midgame with Spirit Shielding. Setup ganks with Dagger & Hex paired with a teammate or solo. Steal jungle inbetween. Past 30min we Leech Boss with Siphon Tempo to stay alive. We play for every minute before they can buy Plated Armor, this hero clocks out when they have it.

hero_build.name: Flubber: The Sequel
hero_build.description:
Version 2.4
Updated to properly reflect new balance changes.
Scroll down on the item screen for build notes. Flubber on!

mod_categories.name: Early Game
mod_categories.description: Sold later: Spirit Strike, High-Velocity Mag, Enduring Spirit, Melee Lifesteal

Puddle Punch deals a melee hit now, so it pays to lean into it early. Spirit Strike and Mystic Burst as your first items will give you nasty harras potential in lane. It also allows you to use more melee hits in combat. HV Mag is great for Viscous' slow projectile speed.

Later on, your main damage will come from good use of your Splatter. Thus, you need to max it ASAP. This is also the skill you want to imbue, and what you should be buying Echo Shard for. If you're confident with Goo Ball, consider Phantom Strike to instantly get on top of people. Ultra late, defenses are up to taste.
